title: Replace an HVAC Air Filter
summary: Replace a disposable HVAC air filter in a home system.
learnerPromise: You will be able to locate, remove, and replace a disposable HVAC air filter in your home.
whyItMatters: A clean air filter helps your heating and cooling system run efficiently, improves indoor air quality, and can lower energy bills. It is a simple maintenance task that most people can do.
realLifeUses: Replacing a clogged filter at the start of a new season
Improving air quality during allergy season
Helping an older HVAC system run more efficiently
Moving into a new home and starting with a fresh filter
materialsNeeded: Replacement air filter (correct size and type)
result: A correctly installed new air filter in the HVAC return vent or furnace.
youWillLearn: How to locate the air filter compartment in your home
How to read the filter size and MERV rating on the old filter frame
How to insert the new filter with the airflow arrows pointing the correct direction
How to set a reminder for the next replacement
miniChallenge: Locate the air filter in your home, check its condition, and replace it with a new one of the correct size.
steps: Turn off the HVAC system at the thermostat before opening the filter compartment.
Locate the filter compartment — usually behind a return air grille or near the furnace.
Slide out the old filter and note the size printed on the cardboard frame.
Insert the new filter with the airflow arrows pointing toward the blower or furnace, not toward the room.
Close the compartment securely, turn the system back on, and set a reminder for the next change.
completionCriteria: New filter is fully seated in the compartment with arrows pointing the correct direction
Compartment door or grille is closed and the HVAC system turns back on normally
commonProblems: New filter does not fit — double-check the size printed on the old filter frame
Not sure which way the arrows point — look for a label on the unit or check the user manual
Filter compartment is hard to open — it may have latches or screws; do not force it
tips: Most filters should be replaced every 1 to 3 months depending on use and air quality
Write the replacement date on the edge of the new filter frame
Higher MERV ratings filter more particles but can restrict airflow — check what your system supports
helpBoundary: Ask for help if the repair feels unsafe or requires a ladder or power tools you are not comfortable using.
